## S4.03 Student Payment {#s4.03-student-payment}

**Owner:** Directors · **Last reviewed:** 2025 board (imported from the Hitchhiker's Guide, July 2026) · **Status:** current — when you find something stale, fix it and update this line.

### Granting Financial Aid Requests {#granting-financial-aid-requests}

You should grant financial aid requests in chunks so that students know that they have received financial aid before the student payment deadline. You also have to grant financial aid in chunks because in order to send payment reminder emails to only the people that haven’t been granted financial (and also not to the people that have already paid), financial aid should be updated. Financial aid should therefore be granted about every 3 days--the same frequency as the payment reminders-- throughout the period after student reg opens and after the student payment/finaid deadlines. 

**NOTE--**there is probably a better way to do this; this is just the solution I found last year, but if you guys can find a better way, then do it\!

Steps to granting financial aid:

1. Open “[Administration Pages](https://yale.learningu.org/admin/)”  
2. Open “Financial Aid Requests” (This is NOT the same as “Financial Aid Grants”)  
3. Look in the “Program” column for the first one that says your program names and start from there  
4. (a) If the student is on Free/Reduced School Lunch (as indicated by a green check mark), their request will be automatically approved, as indicated by the “True” in the “Approved” column.  
   (b) If there is not a green check mark and is instead a red circle, you must now check to see if they’ve written anything in the “Household income” and “Please describe your situation.”  
   (c) If they have written nothing in these columns, then “(none)” will appear. If there is absolutely nothing written in either box, then we assume that the student did not intentionally apply for financial aid, and accidentally just clicked on the tab. This happens A LOT, so don’t be surprised.  
   (d) If the student has written literally anything in EITHER box (ie. its fine if they didn’t fill out their income or if they didn’t provide a reason), even if it’s just “Please grant my financial aid,” then we will grant them financial aid.  
   (e) To grant a student financial aid, click on their username. Under the Percent box of the “Financial aid grant” chart, type in “100” (without the “” marks). Then click “Save” in the bottom right hand corner.  
   (f) Repeat these steps for all students.  
   5\. Now, go back to the Administration Pages, and click on “Financial Aid Grants” (at the top of the page). You must now finalize the financial aid grants. You can do this all at once, so simply click on the checkbox next to the user ID of each student whose financial aid you want to approve. Once you have done this, click on the white box at the bottom of the page on the back “Footer” bar, and choose “Finalize Finaid grants” in the dropdown menu.  
   ![][image8]  
6. Now that the grants are finalized, you have to send emails to all the students telling them that they received financial aid. However, since you will be granting financial aid progressively (ie. you won’t do it all at once, but instead in chunks), you don’t want to send people multiple emails about being approved for financial aid. To avoid this, take the following steps:  
   	IMPORTANT NOTE: If this is the first time during the program you are granting financial aid, you can just follow step 12 and then copy and paste the email addresses into the email provided in 15/16.  
7. First make a copy of [this spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xGWnJp_bF2eqguzHLUV6XSgO9bbwhRAk4iY4yDNIJbs/edit?usp=sharing) for your program. DO NOT just use this template version.  
8. Delete all the info in the username column and delete the data in the columns A-E  
9. Now, from the “Financial Aid Grants” page that you ended on, copy all of the student information for the grants that you have just finalized, and paste it into empty space on the far right so it doesn’t interfere with anything. Now, copy all the usernames below the “H3” where it says “Usernames.” Note that sometimes the first username won’t copy properly, so you have to manually add it. You can delete the rest of the data that isn’t for usernames. Now, you have to find the emails that match up with these usernames.  
10. Go back to the main Splash site. Go to “Manage” and then to your program page. Under “Generate Printables,” go to “Arbitrary User List”  
11. Click “Continue to Select set of Contacts” and then “Students who have been granted financial aid.”  
12. Select “ID” “Username” “Full name” and “Email” and generate the list in HTML format  
13. Hit Cntrl “a” to select the whole output and copy it into columns A-E on the spreadsheet. Delete the “Generated for…” text and move all of the data upwards so it looks like this:  
    ![][image9]  
14. Now, besides each username, each email address should have appeared. Check the first input to make sure it is correct.  
15. Copy all the email addresses and paste them into the “BCC.” then address the email to [yalesplash@gmail.com](mailto:yalesplash@gmail.com). You HAVE to do this or else other people can see the email addresses of the other financial aid people.  
16.  The subject should be “Splash Financial Aid Request Approved” and the body should be something like:  
      
    “Hello,  
      
    Your financial aid request for \_\_\_fall or spring\_\_\_\_ Splash \_\_\_year\_\_ has been approved. You will receive full financial aid. We look forward to seeing you at Splash\!  
      
    Best,  
    Avi, Splash Co-Director 2019”

[image8]: <data:image/png;base64,>
[image9]: <data:image/png;base64,>
